description_long: This is a medium-sized screwdriver featuring a cylindrical, slightly tapered handle in a matte tan color, likely made of plastic or composite material. The handle transitions into a metallic, ribbed grip section that provides additional control and ergonomics for the user. The shaft is metallic and straight, ending in a sharply pointed tip, characteristic of an awl or scratch awl rather than a traditional screwdriver. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. The object appears clean and well-maintained, with no visible signs of wear or damage. Its compact size and robust construction make it suitable for precise manual tasks in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or maintenance settings.
description: A tan-handled, metallic-tipped screwdriver with a ribbed grip and pointed tip, appearing clean and robust. The design is utilitarian and ergonomic, suitable for precise manual tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tan handle, ribbed metallic grip, and pointed metallic tip.
description_view_1: End-on view from the handle side, revealing the circular, hollowed end of the handle and the alignment of the shaft.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the same handle, grip, and pointed tip visible.
description_view_3: End-on view from the tip side, showing the pointed metallic tip and the ribbed grip section.
attribute_handle_type: straight, slightly tapered, plastic, matte finish, ergonomic with a cylindrical shape
attribute_tip_type: awl (pointed tip, not a traditional screwdriver blade)
